mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.4pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index indicate immediate supply chain bottlenecks that trigger short-term equity sell-offs within one trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat following INDPRO declines exceeding 1.0% during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stagnant federal funds rate create real rate compression that forces equity repricing and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 50% of months where CPIAUCSL increases while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a reliable leading indicator of aggregate income expansion, which drives consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with PAYEMS growth over any consecutive 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 750,000 units signals a sudden collapse in construction demand that immediately triggers negative equity market reactions within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of months where HOUST drops below the 750,000 unit threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling industrial production confirms a manufacturing recession that systematically depresses S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in at least 55% of periods where UNRATE rises concurrently with INDPRO declines.
